role_title: Staff Technical Program Manager, Product & Rider Loyalty

location.office_time_required: 3 days per week (Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays); flexibility to work from anywhere up to 4 weeks per year

level_signals: Operates autonomously with only high-level direction
Expected base pay $176K-$220K in SF, consistent with Staff/Senior IC at a major tech company
JD body references 'Senior Technical Program Manager' in description text despite title being 'Staff'

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5-10+ years of experience in a customer-facing product environment, leading cross-functional teams to deliver complex product and platform initiatives iteratively, with multiple dependencies and constraints, in a highly dynamic and agile environment.
Proven ability to operate effectively and autonomously across multiple teams in situations of extreme ambiguity, with only high-level direction.
Experience as an embedded TPM partnering within product engineering teams, with the flexibility to shift into generalist capacity supporting multiple teams concurrently.
Experience building roadmaps, release plans, and program plans with a thorough understanding of dependency management.
Able to communicate highly technical problems and solutions at all levels — from individual contributors to C-level executives.
Experience compiling and presenting business and technology options, backed by data and experimentation, to decision makers.
Lead cross-functional product initiatives from ideation through planning, execution, launch, and post-launch — ensuring alignment, accountability, and measurable outcomes at every phase.

Provide hands-on support across all phases of the software development lifecycle, including project planning, resource allocation, progress tracking, risk mitigation, KPI ownership and measurement, and leadership communications.

Drive loyalty and retention product programs, working closely with product and engineering to define success metrics, prioritize roadmap investments, and accelerate delivery of features that deepen customer engagement and lifetime value.
Lead regulatory and compliance-driven product initiatives, navigating ambiguity and coordinating across legal, policy, product, and engineering stakeholders.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Nice to Have: Experience with loyalty programs, partnerships, pricing models and other key strategic elements that drive product stickiness.

scope.surface: Rideshare Partnerships, Loyalty, and Payments — building a singular loyalty offering for riders and drivers
scope.business_model: B2C rideshare platform (consumer loyalty and retention)
primary_metrics_inferred: KPI ownership and measurement across product initiatives
core_responsibilities: Lead cross-functional product initiatives from ideation through planning, execution, launch, and post-launch

Provide hands-on support across all phases of the software development lifecycle including planning, resource allocation, progress tracking, risk mitigation, KPI ownership, and leadership communications
Partner with engineering, product, data science, design, operations, and other business functions
Drive loyalty and retention product programs, define success metrics, prioritize roadmap investments
Lead regulatory and compliance-driven product initiatives

compensation.equity_form: potential equity offering mentioned but not detailed

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed ($176K-$220K base in SF)
Clear product surface and organizational focus (Partnerships, Loyalty and Payments)
Hybrid schedule is well-defined (3 specific days per week specified)
Generous benefits package including 18 weeks parental leave, 401(k) match, and mental health benefits
Only one item explicitly labeled 'Nice to Have,' so must-haves are well-defined
red_flags: JD title says 'Staff Technical Program Manager' but body copy refers to 'Senior Technical Program Manager' — possible title inconsistency worth clarifying
Role requires in-office 3 days/week in San Francisco only — no remote option for candidates outside SF area
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned anywhere in the JD
Compensation range is for SF area only; other locations not specified
